title: Leptochilus henryi
taxonomy: Leptochilus henryi is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This family of ferns is commonly known as the polypody ferns. The genus Leptochilus is part of the subfamily Polypodioideae. Within the subfamily, the genus Leptochilus is characterized by its unique leaf morphology and distribution. Further classification of Leptochilus henryi within the kingdom and phylum is as a member of the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta.

intro: Leptochilus henryi, also known as Chinese dragon's blood, is a plant species in the genus Leptochilus, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Leptochilus henryi is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leptochilus henryi include tinctures and extracts, which are often used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
